Inputs.evidence: Each request carries ... the reported reason (lost / stolen / damaged)

Outputs.evidence: The customer contacts the bank ... and to request a replacement

What happens.evidence: The customer contacts the bank via the Contact Centre or raises a request in the mobile app, stating the card is lost, stolen, or damaged.

Why it matters.evidence: S. Krause confirmed in the foundational run: Why it matters holds as drafted.

Inputs.evidence: knowledge-based verification (security questions); in the mobile app the customer is already authenticated by login

Why it matters.evidence: S. Krause confirmed in the foundational run: Why it matters holds as drafted.

Inputs.evidence: the agent places an immediate block on the existing card

Outputs.evidence: Card lost or stolen? | Fraud exposure check | Skip to card order

What happens.evidence: The agent places an immediate block on the existing card in the Card Management System so it can no longer be used. For lost or stolen cards the block is permanent; for damaged cards the block is also applied because the card number is reissued.

Why it matters.evidence: S. Krause confirmed in the foundational run: Why it matters holds as drafted.

Inputs.evidence: reviews recent transactions for unauthorised activity

Outputs.evidence: If suspicious transactions are found, see Exception E-2

What happens.evidence: For cards reported lost or stolen, the request is flagged to the Fraud Analyst, who reviews recent transactions for unauthorised activity. Damaged-card requests skip this check.

Why it matters.evidence: S. Krause confirmed in the foundational run: Why it matters holds as drafted.

Outputs.evidence: A like-for-like replacement card of the same product and type as the original

What happens.evidence: The Card Operations Clerk orders a like-for-like replacement card. The new card carries a new card number; the PIN is unchanged and is not reissued.

Why it matters.evidence: S. Krause confirmed in the foundational run: Why it matters holds as drafted.

Inputs.evidence: dispatched to the customer's registered address

Outputs.evidence: The replacement card is produced by the card bureau and dispatched to the customer's registered address.

What happens.evidence: The replacement card is produced by the card bureau and dispatched to the customer's registered address. The customer is told the expected delivery time (5-7 business days) and that the card must be activated on first use.

Why it matters.evidence: S. Krause confirmed in the foundational run: Why it matters holds as drafted.

How it is met.evidence: https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/intro/mip-online/2018/html/1803_revisedpsd.en.html — "the payment service provider must be able to block it" — fetched 2026-05-19

What it requires.evidence: https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/intro/mip-online/2018/html/1803_revisedpsd.en.html — "The maximum amount for which a payer can be made liable ... has been lowered from 150 to 50 euro" — fetched 2026-05-19

How it is met.evidence: https://www.gdprregulation.eu/gdpr-for-financial-services/ — "uphold customers' rights, including access to, rectification, and erasure of personal data" — fetched 2026-05-19

What it requires.evidence: https://www.gdprregulation.eu/gdpr-for-financial-services/ — "Banks must make sure the data they have is correct and up to date, and if there's an error, fix it fast" — fetched 2026-05-19

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.gdprregulation.eu/gdpr-for-financial-services/ — "Banks must adhere to GDPR principles such as lawfulness, fairness, transparency, and data minimization" — fetched 2026-05-19

What it requires.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/publications-and-media/press-releases/eba-launches-consultation-its-draft-guidelines-third-party-risk-management-regard-non-ict-related — "the sound management of third-party risk ... non-ICT related services provided by third-party service providers" — fetched 2026-05-19

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.dlapiper.com/en-us/insights/publications/law-in-tech/from-outsourcing-to-third-party-arrangements-new-eba-guidelines-for-third-party — "any provision by a third party of one or more functions to a financial services entity" — fetched 2026-05-19

How it is met.evidence: https://gdprlocal.com/gdpr-credit-card-data/ — "maintaining secure networks and firewalls, and performing regular vulnerability testing" — fetched 2026-05-19

What it requires.evidence: https://gdprlocal.com/gdpr-credit-card-data/ — "encrypting cardholder data during transmission and storage, implementing strict access controls" — fetched 2026-05-19

Why it applies.evidence: https://gdprlocal.com/gdpr-credit-card-data/ — "Any banking app that processes, stores, or transmits card information must comply with PCI DSS requirements" — fetched 2026-05-19
